heading: Annual modal research plans
label: 6501 Annual modal research plans
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this chapter, referred to in subsec. (c)(2), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 114–94Dec. 4, 2015

Amendments2021—Subsec. (a)(1). , added par. (1) and struck out former par. (1). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than May 1 of each year, the head of each modal administration and joint program office of the Department of Transportation shall submit to the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology of the Department of Transportation (referred to in this chapter as the ‘Assistant Secretary’) a comprehensive annual modal research plan for the upcoming fiscal year and a detailed outlook for the following fiscal year.”Pub. Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2015 Amendment note under , Government Organization and Employees.Oct. 1, 2015section 1003 of Pub. L. 114–94section 5313 of Title 5

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–94, div. A, title VI, § 6019(a)129 Stat. 1577Dec. 4, 2015“Congress finds that—“(1)Federal transportation research planning—“(A)should be coordinated by the Office of the Secretary [of Transportation]; and“(B)should be, to the extent practicable, multimodal and not occur solely within the sub-agencies of the Department [of Transportation];“(2)managing a multimodal research portfolio within the Office of the Secretary will—“(A)help identify opportunities in which research could be applied across modes; and“(B)prevent duplication of efforts and waste of limited Federal resources;“(3)the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology at the Department of Transportation will—“(A)give stakeholders a formal opportunity to address concerns;“(B)ensure unbiased research; and“(C)improve the overall research products of the Department; and“(4)increasing transparency of transportation research and development efforts will—“(A)build stakeholder confidence in the final product; and“(B)lead to the improved implementation of research findings.”

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–94, div. A, title VI, § 6019(a)129 Stat. 1577Dec. 4, 2015“Congress finds that—“(1)Federal transportation research planning—“(A)should be coordinated by the Office of the Secretary [of Transportation]; and“(B)should be, to the extent practicable, multimodal and not occur solely within the sub-agencies of the Department [of Transportation];“(2)managing a multimodal research portfolio within the Office of the Secretary will—“(A)help identify opportunities in which research could be applied across modes; and“(B)prevent duplication of efforts and waste of limited Federal resources;“(3)the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology at the Department of Transportation will—“(A)give stakeholders a formal opportunity to address concerns;“(B)ensure unbiased research; and“(C)improve the overall research products of the Department; and“(4)increasing transparency of transportation research and development efforts will—“(A)build stakeholder confidence in the final product; and“(B)lead to the improved implementation of research findings.”

children.label: A
children.text: a comprehensive annual modal research plan for the following fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: a detailed outlook for the fiscal year thereafter.

children.label: A
children.text: a general description of the strategic goals of the Department that are addressed by the research programs being carried out by the Assistant Secretary or modal administration, as applicable;

children.label: i
children.text: the major objectives of the program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the requested amount of funding for each program and area;

children.label: C
children.text: a list of activities the Assistant Secretary or modal administration plans to carry out under the research programs described in subparagraph (B);

children.label: i
children.text: potential outputs, outcomes, and impacts on technologies and practices used by entities subject to the jurisdiction of the modal administration;

children.label: ii
children.text: potential effects on applicable regulations of the modal administration, including the modification or modernization of those regulations;

children.label: iii
children.text: potential economic or societal impacts; and

children.label: I
children.text: the applicable modal administration; or

children.label: II
children.text: the Department of Transportation;

children.label: i
children.text: institutions of higher education; and

children.label: ii
children.text: private sector entities; and

children.label: F
children.text: such other requirements as the Assistant Secretary considers to be necessary.

children.heading: .—Relationship to strategic plan
children.label: 3 .—Relationship to strategic plan
children.text: Each plan prepared or submitted under paragraph (1), after the plan required in 2016, shall be consistent with the strategic plan developed under section 6503.

children.label: A
children.text: review the scope of the research; and

children.label: i
children.text: approve the plan and outlook; or

children.label: I
children.text: revised in accordance with such suggestions as the Assistant Secretary shall include to ensure conformity with the criteria described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: II
children.text: resubmitted to the Assistant Secretary for approval.

children.label: i
children.text: the plan contains research objectives that are consistent with the strategic research and policy objectives of the Department of Transportation included in the strategic plan required under section 6503; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the research programs described in the plan have the potential to benefit the safety, mobility, and efficiency of the United States transportation system;

children.label: B
children.text: identify and evaluate any potential opportunities for collaboration between or among modal administrations with respect to particular research programs described in the plan;

children.label: C
children.text: identify and evaluate whether other modal administrations may be better suited to carry out the research programs described in the plan;

children.label: i
children.text: duplicative across modal administrations; or

children.label: ii
children.text: unnecessary; and

children.label: E
children.text: take into consideration such other criteria as the Assistant Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.heading: .—Publications
children.label: 3 .—Publications
children.text: Not later than January 30 of each year, the Secretary shall publish on a public website each plan and outlook that has been approved under paragraph (1)(B)(i).

children.heading: .—Rejection of duplicative research efforts
children.label: 4 .—Rejection of duplicative research efforts
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may not approve any plan submitted by the head of a modal administration or joint program office pursuant to subsection (a) if any of the projects described in the plan duplicate significant aspects of research efforts of any other modal administration.

children.heading: .—Savings clause
children.label: 5 .—Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this subsection limits the ability of the head of a modal administration to comply with applicable law.
the research is required by an Act of Congress;
the research was part of a contract that was funded before the date of enactment of this chapter;
the research updates previously commissioned research; or
the Assistant Secretary certifies to Congress that such research is necessary, and provides justification for such certification.

children.label: A
children.text: each modal research plan has been reviewed; and

children.label: B
children.text: there is no duplication of study for research directed, commissioned, or conducted by the Department of Transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: notify Congress of the duplicative research; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to Congress a corrective action plan to eliminate the duplicative research.
